page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 3200 GB local NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results show strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier. Large-block memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 340.0 GB/sec for read-only operations and 160.84 GB/sec for write operations, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is highly competitive, scoring in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and database operations fall into the average tier, reflecting the shared CPU allocation model.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core workloads. Despite this, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial 3200 GB NVMe SSD storage offer high resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive tasks that do not require dedicated CPU cores. This server is well-suited for large-scale caching, memory-mapped databases, data processing pipelines, and web serving workloads that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local storage.
description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Milan processor with a shared CPU allocation. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 3200 GB NVMe SSD. While its shared CPU architecture leads to average multi-core benchmark results, its memory subsystem delivers top-tier performance, highlighted by strong large-block read and write bandwidth. This combination of high memory capacity and fast local storage provides a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads. The instance also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and features a 2.0 GHz CPU clock speed. It is suitable for database operations, caching layers, and web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD, offering high memory density for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
128.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Bundled 3200 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Suited for memory-mapped databases, caching, and web serving
